habitat: Subtropical/tropical moist lowland and montane forests
story: Few birds capture the imagination quite like the Asian Emerald Cuckoo (Chrysococcyx maculatus). As a member of the Cuculidae family, it has carved out a unique niche in Subtropical/tropical moist lowland and montane forests. Its conservation status is Least Concern (LC), a classification that drives crucial research and protection efforts. The Asian Emerald Cuckoo's presence is a vital sign of its ecosystem's health, making its preservation a priority. This poster is a tribute to the Asian Emerald Cuckoo, celebrating its role in the natural world and inspiring a deeper appreciation for biodiversity.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
